message.content:
You are resuming a crashed agent session on the bim-ai repo
  (/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai). bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring
  tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).

  REPO ORIENTATION (WP-C relevant paths):
  - packages/web/src/plan/StairBySketchCanvas.tsx — stair-by-sketch UI
  - packages/web/src/plan/StairSketchEditor.tsx — stair sketch editor
  - packages/web/src/plan/stairAutobalance.ts — auto-balance run widths
  - packages/web/src/plan/stairPlanSymbol.ts — stair plan symbol
  - packages/web/src/plan/rampPlanSymbol.ts — ramp plan symbol (already exists!)
  - packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ramp.ts — ramp 3D mesh (already exists!)
  - packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ramp.test.ts — ramp tests (already exists!)
  - packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.multiRunStair.ts — multi-storey stair mesh
  - packages/web/src/levels/LevelStack.tsx — level management UI
  - packages/web/src/clipboard/copyToLevels.ts — paste-to-levels command (done)
  - packages/web/src/clipboard/copyPaste.ts — Ctrl+C/V clipboard
  - packages/core/src/index.ts — shared Element types

  Architecture:
  - Elements stored as plain objects with a kind discriminator ('stair', 'ramp', 'level').
  - Semantic commands: onSemanticCommand({ type: 'createRamp', ... }).
  - 3D meshes: viewport/meshBuilders.ts dispatches to sub-builders by element kind.
  - Plan symbols: separate file per type, called from plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts.
  - Tests: co-located *.test.ts, run with pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web.
  - Prettier runs automatically.

  WHAT WAS DONE BEFORE THE CRASH:
  - C1 Ramp: PARTIAL — meshBuilders.ramp.ts + rampPlanSymbol.ts + tests exist; 'ramp' ToolId is in toolRegistry and ramp kind is in core/index.ts. Grammar/inspector completeness unknown — verify before
  adding.
  - C2 Multi-storey stair: PARTIAL — meshBuilders.multiRunStair.ts has multiStorey support; meshBuilders.multiStoreyStair.test.ts exists
  - C6 copyToLevels command: DONE — clipboard/copyToLevels.ts + tests
  C3, C4, C5, C7 have no visible committed work — assume not started.

  BEFORE WRITING ANY CODE, run:
    git pull --rebase origin main
    pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web -- ramp
    pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web -- multiStorey

  Read these files to understand actual current state:
  - plan/rampPlanSymbol.ts — check what plan symbol geometry it produces
  - viewport/meshBuilders.ramp.ts — check if mesh is complete or has stubs
  - tools/toolGrammar.ts — search for 'ramp' to see if grammar exists
  - levels/LevelStack.tsx — understand the current level UI before C5

  WHAT STILL NEEDS TO BE DONE:

  C1 — Ramp Tool: complete grammar + inspector
  Files exist. Verify and complete:
  1. Does toolGrammar.ts have a ramp grammar? If not, add:
     - Mode "By Length": click insertion point → click endpoint. Rise = topLevel.elevationMm − baseLevel.elevationMm.
     - Mode "By Sketch": draw boundary polygon + run line inside it.
     - Options bar: mode toggle, widthMm, topLevelId picker.
  2. Inspector: widthMm, runMm, slopePercent (read-only computed), material, hasRailingLeft/Right, topLevelId
  3. Advisor: if slopePercent > 8.33 (1:12 max accessibility slope), emit a warning advisory
  4. Fix any TODOs/stubs in rampPlanSymbol.ts or meshBuilders.ramp.ts
  5. Verify all ramp tests pass

  C2 — Multi-Storey Stair: complete and test
  meshBuilders.multiRunStair.ts has the logic. Verify and complete:
  1. stair element has topLevelId and multiStorey: true fields in core/index.ts
  2. Grammar: after first-floor stair is sketched, "Extend to level?" option appears in options bar
  3. Plan symbol: on each intermediate level's plan view, stair symbol clipped to that level's run segment
  4. Schedule: stairScheduleEvidenceReadout.ts shows full height for multi-storey stairs
  5. Tests: 3-storey stair — mesh vertex count = 3 × single-storey; plan symbol appears on each level

  C3 — Complex Stair Configurations
  Audit StairBySketchCanvas.tsx and stairAutobalance.ts for:
  1. L-shape (2 runs + 90° landing): verify sketch editor accepts L-shaped boundary, mesh handles non-rectangular landings
  2. U-shape (3 runs + 2 landings): same audit
  3. Winder stairs: add winderAtCorner: boolean to stair element — triangular treads at corner instead of landing
  4. Spiral stair: new element type stair_spiral with centrePtXMm, centrePtYMm, innerRadiusMm, outerRadiusMm, totalAngleDeg, treadCount.
     Plan symbol: concentric arcs + radial lines.
     3D mesh: meshBuilders.spiralStair.ts — rotate each tread by totalAngleDeg/treadCount around centre with incremental Z.
  Tests for each: verify no geometry gaps (correct normals, no open mesh edges)

  C4 — Attach Top/Base of Walls to Roof or Floor (not started)
  1. Selection action "Attach Top" when ≥1 wall selected: Step 1 = walls; Step 2 = click the roof/floor
  2. Semantic command: { type: 'attachWallTop', wallId, hostId }. Sets topConstraint: { hostId, hostFace: 'bottom' } on wall. The CSG system (csgWallBaseGeometry.ts) already handles trimming — this just sets
   the relationship.
  3. "Detach Top/Base" clears the constraint
  4. In 3D: wall silhouette follows roof slope. In plan: wall top line shows as dashed.
  Tests: attach gable-end wall to hip roof, verify mesh top vertices lie on roof surface

  C5 — Add Multiple Levels (not started)
  In levels/LevelStack.tsx add:
  - "Add Multiple…" button (or right-click on level: "Add N Levels Above")
  - Small dialog: count (default 3), spacing (default 3000mm), name prefix (default "Ebene")
  - Dispatches N createLevel commands sequentially
  Tests: add 4 levels at 2800mm spacing, verify correct elevations

  C6 — Clipboard UI (complete)
  copyToLevels.ts command done. Wire the UI:
  - Ctrl+C on selection stores element IDs + current level in clipboardStore
  - Ctrl+V pastes at cursor — check clipboard/copyPaste.ts for existing logic
  - "Paste Aligned to Selected Levels" in selection toolbar → level picker modal → dispatches copyToLevels

  C7 — Stair Railing Completeness Pass
  Verify and complete:
  1. When a stair is created, auto-create railing elements for each open side with hostStairId set
  2. Stair move → railings move; stair delete → railings deleted
  3. Inspector on railing: hostStairId (read-only), railingHeight, topRailProfile, balustradeSpacingMm
  Tests: create stair, verify railings auto-created; delete stair, verify railings removed

  RULES:
  - git pull --rebase origin main before every major sub-task
  - Commit + push after each completed sub-task
  - Do NOT touch annotation/dimension, export, phase dialogs, groups, structural beam/brace, or massing files
  - toolRegistry.ts is shared — always rebase before adding entries
  - pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web before each push
  - Update spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md as you complete items

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

attachment.snippet:
1	/**
2	 * Per-tool grammar — spec §16.4 / §16.5.
3	 *
4	 * Each tool's interaction model is captured here as plain data so the
5	 * tool can be unit-tested without DOM. The shape is consistent across
6	 * tools so the canvas can switch on `kind` and dispatch input.
7	 */
8	
9	export type { WallLocationLine } from '@bim-ai/core';
10	import type { WallLocationLine } from '@bim-ai/core';
11	
12	/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
13	/* EDT-06 — Tool grammar polish (Chain / Multiple / Tag-on-Place /        */
14	/*           Numeric input). The canvas wires the per-tool reducers in    */
15	/*           this file plus a top-level `ToolGrammarModifiers` block      */
16	/*           that feeds the Options Bar.                                  */
17	/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
18	
19	/** Modifiers that the canvas Options Bar exposes to the user. */
20	export interface ToolGrammarModifiers {
21	  /** Place Wall continues from the last endpoint until Esc / different tool. */
22	  chainable: boolean;
23	  /** Insert Door / Window stays in tool until Esc; otherwise exits after first placement. */
24	  multipleable: boolean;
25	  /** During wall / door / window placement, auto-place a tag of the configured family. */

27	  /** Numeric input mode: typing a digit while drawing pops a numeric input field. */

39	  wallDrawRadiusMm: number | null;
40	}

190	/**
191	 * Numeric-input field state — appears at the cursor while a numeric-capable
192	 * tool is mid-draw and the user types a digit. `axis` toggles on Tab so a
193	 * second number can drive the perpendicular direction.
194	 */
195	export interface NumericInputState {
196	  active: boolean;
197	  value: string;
198	  axis: 'primary' | 'perpendicular';
199	}
200	
201	export function initialNumericInputState(): NumericInputState {
202	  return { active: false, value: '', axis: 'primary' };
203	}
204	
205	export type NumericInputEvent =
206	  | { kind: 'start'; firstDigit: string }
207	  | { kind: 'append'; digit: string }
208	  | { kind: 'backspace' }
209	  | { kind: 'tab-axis' }
210	  | { kind: 'commit' }
211	  | { kind: 'cancel' };
212	
213	export function reduceNumericInput(
214	  state: NumericInputState,
215	  event: NumericInputEvent,
216	): NumericInputState {
217	  switch (event.kind) {
218	    case 'start':
219	      return { active: true, value: event.firstDigit, axis: 'primary' };
220	    case 'append':
221	      if (!state.active) return state;
222	      return { ...state, value: state.value + event.digit };
223	    case 'backspace':
224	      if (!state.active) return state;
225	      return { ...state, value: state.value.slice(0, -1) };
226	    case 'tab-axis':
227	      if (!state.active) return state;
228	      return {
229	        ...state,
230	        axis: state.axis === 'primary' ? 'perpendicular' : 'primary',
231	      };
232	    case 'commit':
233	    case 'cancel':
234	      return initialNumericInputState();
235	  }
236	}

attachment.snippet:
1	# Revit 2026 Feature-Parity Tracker
2	
3	Last updated: 2026-05-16
4	Source: Detlef Ridder — *Autodesk Revit 2026: Der umfassende Praxiseinstieg für Architekturkonstruktion*, mitp 2026 (ISBN 978-3-7475-1101-5)
5	
6	Purpose: exhaustive chapter-by-chapter comparison between Revit 2026 (as taught in the book) and what bim-ai currently supports. Every leaf section of the table of contents becomes a row. The goal is to expose gaps at maximum granularity so engineering work can be scoped and prioritised.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Status Legend
11	
12	- `Done` — fully implemented, tested, and working at production quality
13	- `Partial` — some sub-capability exists but parity is incomplete
14	- `Not Started` — no implementation exists
15	- `N/A` — not applicable to a browser-based BIM SaaS (installation flows, desktop-only licensing, Windows hardware checks, etc.)
16	
17	Priority:
18	- `P0` — core authoring workflow; blocks architects from meaningful use
19	- `P1` — important for professional parity; significantly limits daily use if absent
20	- `P2` — useful but architects can work around it today
21	- `P3` — niche, advanced, or rarely used in typical architectural projects
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Chapter 1 — Revit installieren, starten und loslegen (UI & startup)
26	
27	### 1.1 Die Testversion (trial licensing)
28	**Status: N/A**
29	Revit requires download and 30-day activation. bim-ai is browser-based with its own subscription/trial model. No parity gap.
30	
31	### 1.2 Die Studentenversion (student licensing)
32	**Status: N/A**
33	Autodesk-specific educational licensing. bim-ai has its own account system.
34	
35	### 1.3 Hard- und Software-Voraussetzungen (system requirements)
36	**Status: N/A**
37	Revit 2026 requires 64-bit Windows 10/11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 11 GPU, 30 GB disk. bim-ai runs in a browser. No parity gap.
38	
39	### 1.4 Installierte Programme (installed components)
40	**Status: N/A**
41	Revit installs Revit main, Viewer, Add-Ins Manager, Worksharing Monitor. bim-ai is a web app.
42	
43	### 1.5 Revit starten (startup & project templates)
44	**Status: Partial — P2**
45	Revit shows a start screen with recently-used projects, a choice of project template (None, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau vereinfacht, BIM Gebäudetechnik) and a new-project dialog. bim-ai has a project creation flow and project list, and now:
46	- Project templates (projectTemplates.ts) implemented: minimal, residential (Wohnbau), commercial (Gewerbebau) with level + phase setup commands. Template picker added to project creation dialog (ProjectSetupDialog.tsx, "Templates" section).
47	- Template picker allows selecting a template and applying its createLevel/createPhase commands in one click.
48	Still missing:
49	- No multi-template selection at project creation time (only one applied at a time)
50	- No "Revit 2026 start page" visual freshness (recently-used thumbnails, cloud projects)
51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94	
95	#### 1.6.6 Benutzung der Werkzeuge (how tools activate, options bar, modify ribbon)
96	**Status: Partial — P1**
97	bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.

100	**Status: Partial — P1**

Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
102	
103	#### 1.6.8 Optionsleiste: wichtigste Exemplareigenschaften (options bar for active tool)
104	**Status: Partial — P1**
105	OptionsBar.tsx exists and is wired for wall placement (location line, chain mode, offset, radius). Not all tools have fully populated options bars as in Revit.
106	
107	#### 1.6.9 Statusleiste (status bar with command hints)
108	**Status: Partial — P2**
109	bim-ai shows contextual hints during active tool operations. Full Revit-style status bar with structured "Select element" / "Click to place" strings per tool state is not fully implemented.
110	
111	#### 1.6.10 Ansichtssteuerung (view controls: scale, detail level, visual style, shadows, crop)
112	**Status: Partial — P1**
113	bim-ai has:
114	- Plan detail level toolbar (PlanDetailLevelToolbar.tsx — Done)
115	- Visual style selection (renderStyles.ts — Done)
116	- Sun/shadow toggle (SunOverlay.tsx — Done)
117	- Scale display: Partial (shown in plan header)
118	Missing: per-view crop region editing, view-level visibility/graphics overrides dialog, thin-lines toggle ("Feine Linien"), hide/isolate element commands.
119	
120	#### 1.6.11 Projektbrowser (project browser tree: views, sheets, families, groups, Revit links)
121	**Status: Partial — P1**
122	bim-ai has a workspace/project browser concept. Plan views, 3D views, section views, sheet views are accessible via tabs/navigation. Missing:

aiTitle: Resume bim-ai stair and ramp feature work

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user asked to resume a crashed agent session on the bim-ai repo. The goal was to continue implementing Revit 2026 parity work package C (WP-C) which covers: C1 Ramp Tool, C2 Multi-Storey Stair, C3 Complex Stair Configurations, C4 Attach Top/Base of Walls to Roof, C5 Add Multiple Levels, C6 Clipboard UI, and C7 Stair Railing Completeness.

   Specific instructions:
   - Run `git pull --rebase origin main` before every major sub-task
   - Commit + push after each completed sub-task
   - Do NOT touch annotation/dimension, export, phase dialogs, groups, structural beam/brace, or massing files (these had already been worked on by the crashed agent)
   - toolRegistry.ts is shared — always rebase before adding entries
   - pnpm test before each push
   - Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as items complete

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - bim-ai: browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest)
   - Elements stored as plain objects with a `kind` discriminator
   - Semantic commands: `onSemanticCommand({ type: 'createRamp', ... })`
   - 3D meshes: `viewport/meshBuilders.ts` dispatches to sub-builders by element kind
   - Plan symbols: separate file per type, called from `plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts`
   - Tests: co-located `*.test.ts`, run with `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`
   - Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write via Claude Code PostToolUse hook
   - `@testing-library/react` used for component tests; does NOT have jest-dom extended matchers — must use `element.textContent.toContain()` not `toHaveTextContent()`, and `.toBeTruthy()` not `.toBeInTheDocument()`
   - `afterEach(() => { cleanup(); })` required in all React component test files to prevent cross-test DOM pollution

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts`** (modified)
     - Critical fix: added import and re-export of `makeRampMesh` so test could import it from `'./meshBuilders'`
     - Also dispatches for `beam_system`, `brace`, `mass_box`, `mass_extrusion`, `mass_revolution` elements added
     ```typescript
     import { makeRampMesh } from './meshBuilders.ramp';
     export { makeRampMesh };
     ```

   - **`packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts`** (modified)
     - Added 'ramp' to `TOOL_CAPABILITIES`
     - Added `BeamSystemState` + `reduceBeamSystem` state machine for sketch-closed-boundary workflow
     ```typescript
     ramp: { chainable: false, multipleable: false, tagOnPlace: false, numericInput: true },
     ```

   - **`packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ramp.ts`** (existing, complete)
     - Builds sloped 3D ramp surface with 6 faces (top + underside + 4 sides), optional railing lines
     - ADA slope warning when `slopePercent > 8.33`
     - All 6 tests now pass

   - **`packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ramp.test.ts`** (existing, fixed)
     - Previously failed because it imported `makeRampMesh` from `'./meshBuilders'` (the hub file) but the function was only in `'./meshBuilders.ramp'`
     - Fix: re-export from hub

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/rampPlanSymbol.ts`** (existing, complete)
     - Draws outline + diagonal uphill arrows in plan view

   - **`packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.multiRunStair.ts`** (existing, complete)
     - Handles L-shape, U-shape, winder (winderAtCorner=true), spiral (shape='spiral'), sketch-path, multi-storey stairs
     - `collectFloorElevations()` finds intermediate levels and stacks geometry per floor segment

   - **`packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.multiStoreyStair.test.ts`** (existing, passing)
     - 2 tests: 3-storey stair has more children than single-storey; bounding box max Y ≈ 6.0m

   - **`packages/web/src/levels/LevelStack.tsx`** (existing, complete)
     - Has "Add Multiple…" button, dialog with Count/Spacing/Prefix state, `handleCreateMultiple` dispatch
     - `onCreateMultipleLevels` prop accepts `Array<{ name: string; elevationMm: number }>`

   - **`packages/web/src/levels/LevelStack.test.tsx`** (existing, 6 passing tests)
     - Tests: button visibility, dialog open/close, correct elevations for 4 levels at 2800mm spacing

   - **`packages/web/src/phases/PhaseManagerDialog.tsx`** (new from prior session)
     - Phase management CRUD dialog: sequence table, add/rename/reorder/delete phases

   - **`packages/web/src/phases/PhaseManagerDialog.test.tsx`** (new, fixed)
     - Fixed: added `afterEach(cleanup)`, replaced `toHaveTextContent`/`toBeInTheDocument` with standard vitest assertions
     - 8 tests now pass

   - **`packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectInfoDialog.tsx`** (new from prior session)
     - Project Information 8-field form (name, number, client, status, author, issue date, check date, description, address)

   - **`packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectInfoDialog.test.tsx`** (new, fixed)
     - Fixed: replaced `toHaveValue` with `(element as HTMLInputElement).value`
     - 5 tests pass

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/structuralValidation.ts`** (new from prior session)
     - `validateBoundary`, `findDuplicateWalls`, `findOrphanedHostedElements`, `validateHostedElementSpans`, `runStructuralValidation`

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/modifyAvailability.ts`** (new from prior session)
     - Element availability checks for modify operations

   - **`packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.beamSystem.ts`** (new from prior session)
     - Beam system 3D mesh builder

   - **`packages/web/src/clipboard/copyPaste.ts`** (existing, complete)
     - Has `copyToLevels` and `pasteAlignedToLevels` functions
     - Ctrl+C/V wired in PlanCanvas.tsx (lines ~4805-4841)

   - **`packages/web/src/clipboard/copyToLevels.ts`** (existing, complete)
     - `CopyToLevelsCommand` type, `filterValidTargetLevels`, `levelOffsetMm`, `buildCopyToLevelsCommands`

   - **`packages/web/src/families/geometryFns/stairRailingAutoCreate.ts`** (existing)
     - Auto-creates railing elements for each open side of a stair with `hostStairId` set
     - 7 tests passing

   - **`spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md`** (updated)
     - §2.9.3 Complex Stair: Partial → Done (G1)
     - §3.3.3 Clipboard: updated with Ctrl+C/V wired status
     - §8.5.2 Multiple Levels: Not Started → Done (G1)
     - §8.6.5 Multi-Storey Stair: Partial → Done (G1)
     - §8.8 Ramps: Not Started → Done (G1)

   - **`packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts`** (modified by prior session)
     - Added 'beam-system' ToolId and definition

   - **`packages/web/src/tools/authoringCommandContract.ts`** (modified)
     - Added 'beam-system' contract entry

   - **`packages/core/src/index.ts`** (modified)
     - Added `leader_text` ElemKind + Element union variant
     - Added `description?: string | null` to phase element type

   - **`packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx`** (modified)
     - Wired PhaseManagerDialog with `phaseManagerOpen` state
     - `onManagePhases` handler

   - **`packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectMenu.tsx`** (modified)
     - Added `onManagePhases` prop + "Manage Phases…" menu item

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx`** (modified)
     - Wired BeamSystemState machine
     - Ctrl+C/V keyboard handlers at lines ~4805-4841

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/detailComponentsRender.ts`** (modified)

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/SheetCanvas.tsx`** (modified)
     - `resolveTokenValue` now reads `titleblockParameters` + `project_settings` element for token fallback chain

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Git rebase corruption**: The `.git/rebase-merge` directory was missing but git thought rebase was in progress. The cherry-pick abort + rebase continue eventually worked partially. Resolved by using `git merge origin/main` approach was blocked; eventually `git rebase --continue` in the in-progress state worked.
   - **`makeRampMesh is not a function`**: Test imported from `'./meshBuilders'` but function was only in `'./meshBuilders.ramp'`. Fixed by adding `import { makeRampMesh } from './meshBuilders.ramp'; export { makeRampMesh };` to `meshBuilders.ts`.
   - **jest-dom matchers not available**: Tests used `toHaveTextContent`, `toBeInTheDocument`, `toHaveValue` which are `@testing-library/jest-dom` matchers not configured in vitest. Fixed by replacing with: `.textContent.toContain()`, `.toBeTruthy()`, `(el as HTMLInputElement).value`.
   - **"Found multiple elements" in tests**: Tests lacked `afterEach(() => { cleanup(); })`, causing DOM pollution between tests. Fixed by adding cleanup import and afterEach hook to PhaseManagerDialog.test.tsx and ProjectInfoDialog.test.tsx.
   - **structuralValidation tests appeared to fail**: Initial run showed 2 failures but after fixing the PhaseManagerDialog cleanup issue, re-running with `--reporter=verbose` showed all 17 tests pass.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Diagnosed crashed agent session by checking git log, diff, and untracked files
   - Determined which WP-C sub-tasks were already done vs needing work
   - Fixed the critical ramp test failure (import/export issue)
   - Fixed all jest-dom matcher issues in new test files from the crashed session
   - Verified all prior session work was solid before committing
   - Organized commits cleanly (ramp fix, phases, beam/mass, project info, tracker updates)
   - All 109+ tests across relevant directories pass

6. All user messages:
   - The user provided a long crash-recovery prompt with: repo orientation, what was done before crash, what still needs doing (C1-C7), rules for the agent to follow. Key rules:
     - "git pull --rebase origin main before every major sub-task"
     - "Commit + push after each completed sub-task"
     - "Do NOT touch annotation/dimension, export, phase dialogs, groups, structural beam/brace, or massing files"
     - "toolRegistry.ts is shared — always rebase before adding entries"
     - "pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web before each push"
     - "Update spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md as you complete items"
   - The second user message was the compaction request (TEXT ONLY, no tools).

7. Pending Tasks:

The missing piece is a "Paste Aligned to Selected Levels" button in the selection toolbar (or a Cmd+K command) that opens a level picker modal and dispatches `copyElementsToLevels` commands via `buildCopyToLevelsCommands`. This is the only remaining WP-C item.

8. Current Work:

   The last substantive action was updating `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` and committing tracker updates.

9. Optional Next Step:
   The only remaining task from WP-C is implementing the "Paste Aligned to Selected Levels" UI for C6. From the crash-recovery prompt:
   
   > "C6 — Clipboard UI (complete)
   > copyToLevels.ts command done. Wire the UI:

   > - Ctrl+V pastes at cursor — check clipboard/copyPaste.ts for existing logic ✓ (already done)

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

it('covers every modal/dialog command with canonical trigger ownership — UX-RISK-005', () => {
    const dialogTriggers = new Map<string, readonly string[]>([
      ['project.restore-snapshot', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
      ['project.share-presentation', ['cmd-k', 'header']],
      ['project.manage-links', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
      ['project.import.ifc', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
      ['project.import.dxf', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
      ['library.open-family', ['cmd-k', 'ribbon', 'secondary-sidebar']],
      ['library.open-material-browser', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar', 'element-sidebar']],
      ['library.open-appearance-asset-browser', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar', 'element-sidebar']],
      ['help.keyboard-shortcuts', ['cmd-k']],
      ['help.replay-onboarding-tour', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
      ['advisor.open', ['cmd-k', 'footer']],
      ['jobs.open', ['cmd-k', 'footer']],
      ['milestone.open', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
      ['advisor.apply-first-fix', ['cmd-k', 'dialog']],
      ['sheet.export-share', ['cmd-k', 'ribbon', 'header']],
      ['visibility.plan.graphics', ['cmd-k', 'ribbon', 'secondary-sidebar']],
    ] as const);
    const modalCapabilities = getAllCommandCapabilities()
      .filter((capability) => capability.executionSurface === 'modal')
      .sort((a, b) => a.id.localeCompare(b.id));

    expect(modalCapabilities.map((capability) => capability.id)).toEqual(
      [...dialogTriggers.keys()].sort(),
    );
    for (const capability of modalCapabilities) {
      expect(capability.surfaces, capability.id).toEqual(dialogTriggers.get(capability.id));
      expect(capability.surfaces, capability.id).toContain('cmd-k');
    }
  });

  it('supports direct 3D drafting for core hosted and structural tools', () => {
    const direct3dToolIds = Object.values(getToolRegistry(tIdentity))
      .filter((tool) => tool.modes.includes('3d'))
      .map((tool) => tool.id);

    expect(new Set(direct3dToolIds)).toEqual(
      new Set([
        'select',
        'wall',
        'door',
        'window',
        'floor',
        'roof',
        'stair',
        'railing',
        'room',
        'area',

3	export type AuthoringCommandKind =
4	  | 'line'
5	  | 'sketch'
6	  | 'hosted'
7	  | 'point'
8	  | 'modify'
9	  | 'review'
10	  | 'document'
11	  | 'resource';
12	
13	export type AuthoringCompletionBehavior =
14	  | 'select-after-commit'
15	  | 'remain-active-while-looping'
16	  | 'explicit-finish'
17	  | 'stay-active'
18	  | 'selection-only'
19	  | 'opens-resource';
20	
21	export interface AuthoringCommandContract {
22	  toolId: ToolId;
23	  kind: AuthoringCommandKind;
24	  validModes: WorkspaceMode[];
25	  requiredContext: string[];
26	  activeOptions: string[];
27	  previewSemantics: string;
28	  completionBehavior: AuthoringCompletionBehavior;
29	  defaultAfterCancel: 'select';
30	}

attachment.snippet:
1	# Revit 2026 Feature-Parity Tracker
2	
3	Last updated: 2026-05-16
4	Source: Detlef Ridder — *Autodesk Revit 2026: Der umfassende Praxiseinstieg für Architekturkonstruktion*, mitp 2026 (ISBN 978-3-7475-1101-5)
5	
6	Purpose: exhaustive chapter-by-chapter comparison between Revit 2026 (as taught in the book) and what bim-ai currently supports. Every leaf section of the table of contents becomes a row. The goal is to expose gaps at maximum granularity so engineering work can be scoped and prioritised.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Status Legend
11	
12	- `Done` — fully implemented, tested, and working at production quality
13	- `Partial` — some sub-capability exists but parity is incomplete
14	- `Not Started` — no implementation exists
15	- `N/A` — not applicable to a browser-based BIM SaaS (installation flows, desktop-only licensing, Windows hardware checks, etc.)
16	
17	Priority:
18	- `P0` — core authoring workflow; blocks architects from meaningful use
19	- `P1` — important for professional parity; significantly limits daily use if absent
20	- `P2` — useful but architects can work around it today
21	- `P3` — niche, advanced, or rarely used in typical architectural projects
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Chapter 1 — Revit installieren, starten und loslegen (UI & startup)
26	
27	### 1.1 Die Testversion (trial licensing)
28	**Status: N/A**
29	Revit requires download and 30-day activation. bim-ai is browser-based with its own subscription/trial model. No parity gap.
30	
31	### 1.2 Die Studentenversion (student licensing)
32	**Status: N/A**
33	Autodesk-specific educational licensing. bim-ai has its own account system.
34	
35	### 1.3 Hard- und Software-Voraussetzungen (system requirements)
36	**Status: N/A**
37	Revit 2026 requires 64-bit Windows 10/11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 11 GPU, 30 GB disk. bim-ai runs in a browser. No parity gap.
38	
39	### 1.4 Installierte Programme (installed components)
40	**Status: N/A**
41	Revit installs Revit main, Viewer, Add-Ins Manager, Worksharing Monitor. bim-ai is a web app.
42	
43	### 1.5 Revit starten (startup & project templates)
44	**Status: Partial — P2**
45	Revit shows a start screen with recently-used projects, a choice of project template (None, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau vereinfacht, BIM Gebäudetechnik) and a new-project dialog. bim-ai has a project creation flow and project list, and now:
46	- Project templates (projectTemplates.ts) implemented: minimal, residential (Wohnbau), commercial (Gewerbebau) with level + phase setup commands. Template picker added to project creation dialog (ProjectSetupDialog.tsx, "Templates" section).
47	- Template picker allows selecting a template and applying its createLevel/createPhase commands in one click.
48	Still missing:
49	- No multi-template selection at project creation time (only one applied at a time)
50	- No "Revit 2026 start page" visual freshness (recently-used thumbnails, cloud projects)
51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94	
95	#### 1.6.6 Benutzung der Werkzeuge (how tools activate, options bar, modify ribbon)
96	**Status: Partial — P1**
97	bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
98	
99	#### 1.6.7 Exemplar- und Typeigenschaften, neue Elementtypen (instance vs type properties, type duplication)
100	**Status: Partial — P1**
101	bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
102	
103	#### 1.6.8 Optionsleiste: wichtigste Exemplareigenschaften (options bar for active tool)
104	**Status: Partial — P1**
105	OptionsBar.tsx exists and is wired for wall placement (location line, chain mode, offset, radius). Not all tools have fully populated options bars as in Revit.
106	
107	#### 1.6.9 Statusleiste (status bar with command hints)
108	**Status: Partial — P2**
109	bim-ai shows contextual hints during active tool operations. Full Revit-style status bar with structured "Select element" / "Click to place" strings per tool state is not fully implemented.
110	
111	#### 1.6.10 Ansichtssteuerung (view controls: scale, detail level, visual style, shadows, crop)
112	**Status: Partial — P1**
113	bim-ai has:
114	- Plan detail level toolbar (PlanDetailLevelToolbar.tsx — Done)
115	- Visual style selection (renderStyles.ts — Done)
116	- Sun/shadow toggle (SunOverlay.tsx — Done)
117	- Scale display: Partial (shown in plan header)
118	Missing: per-view crop region editing, view-level visibility/graphics overrides dialog, thin-lines toggle ("Feine Linien"), hide/isolate element commands.
119	
120	#### 1.6.11 Projektbrowser (project browser tree: views, sheets, families, groups, Revit links)
121	**Status: Partial — P1**
122	bim-ai has a workspace/project browser concept. Plan views, 3D views, section views, sheet views are accessible via tabs/navigation. Missing:

... [1043 lines truncated] ...

content.text:
Pushed. C6 is complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**C6 — Paste Aligned to Selected Levels (Done)**

- **`PasteToLevelsDialog.tsx`**: Level-picker modal that lists all levels except the active one (sorted by elevation), allows multi-select via checkboxes, and dispatches one `copyElementsToLevels` semantic command per checked target level. Closes on Escape or overlay click.

- **8 tests** covering: null-when-closed, level filtering (active level excluded), empty-state message, confirm-button disabled state, dispatch shape (type/sourceLevelId/targetLevelIds/elementIds), Escape key, overlay click, and multi-level dispatch count.

- **Capability graph**: Added `clipboard.paste-to-levels` entry (`executionSurface: 'modal'`, `intendedModes: ['plan']`, `preconditions: ['has-selection']`). Added to `commandCapabilities.test.ts` modal whitelist (UX-RISK-005).

- **`PaletteContext`**: Added `openPasteToLevels?` callback; wired in `Workspace.tsx` alongside `PhaseManagerDialog`.

- **Fix**: Added missing `column-at-grids` `AuthoringCommandContract` that was causing a null-ref crash in uxAudit when `buildToolCapabilities()` iterated all registered tools.

All WP-C items (C1–C7) are now Done.

content.content:
278:Elements can be copied (copy tool) and moved between levels. Revit's "Copy to Clipboard" + "Paste Aligned to Selected Levels" batch workflow for duplicating a full floor to multiple levels is not implemented as a single command.
351:#### 3.3.3 Gruppe »Zwischenablage« (clipboard: cut, copy, paste, paste aligned)
353:Ctrl+C copies selection to clipboard (copyElementsToClipboard); Ctrl+V pastes at cursor (pasteFromOSClipboard) — both wired in PlanCanvas.tsx. copyToLevels / pasteAlignedToLevels helpers implemented with 6 unit tests. "Paste Aligned to Selected Levels" available via Cmd+K (clipboard.paste-to-levels) and implemented as PasteToLevelsDialog with 8 tests.
354:- **C6:** Ctrl+C/V wired. copyToLevels.ts + pasteAlignedToLevels fully implemented. PasteToLevelsDialog (Cmd+K → level-picker modal → dispatches copyElementsToLevels per target) complete with 8 passing tests.
798:`column-at-grids` tool added to toolRegistry (hotkey CAG, plan-only). Grammar: `ColumnAtGridsState` state machine in toolGrammar.ts; PlanCanvas.tsx wires click→toggleGrid (via raycaster pick) and Enter→confirm→`columnPositionsAtGridIntersections`→N `createColumn` commands. `columnAtGrids.ts` helper (gridLineIntersection + columnPositionsAtGridIntersections) with 5 tests. Missing: options bar UI button, visual highlight of selected grids.
959:Client-side CSV export implemented in `export/csvExporter.ts`. Copy to Clipboard also supported. Server-side CSV was already in place; client-side `generateCsv`, `downloadCsv`, and `copyCsvToClipboard` functions added. A "Copy" button added to `SchedulePanel.tsx` next to the existing CSV button.

51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94	
95	#### 1.6.6 Benutzung der Werkzeuge (how tools activate, options bar, modify ribbon)
96	**Status: Partial — P1**
97	bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
98	
99	#### 1.6.7 Exemplar- und Typeigenschaften, neue Elementtypen (instance vs type properties, type duplication)
100	**Status: Partial — P1**
101	bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.

103	#### 1.6.8 Optionsleiste: wichtigste Exemplareigenschaften (options bar for active tool)
104	**Status: Partial — P1**
105	OptionsBar.tsx exists and is wired for wall placement (location line, chain mode, offset, radius). Not all tools have fully populated options bars as in Revit.
106

108	**Status: Partial — P2**

110	
111	#### 1.6.10 Ansichtssteuerung (view controls: scale, detail level, visual style, shadows, crop)

115	- Visual style selection (renderStyles.ts — Done)
